narrative.headline: Power train tops 2020 Ford Fusion owner complaints
narrative.summary: Of 173 owner complaints filed with NHTSA, the power train is the most reported area at 65 reports (38%), followed by the engine at 47 reports (27%) and the electrical system at 37 reports (21%). Most reports describe no crash or fire, but a few do: steering has 3 crashes, and both the electrical system and the fuel/propulsion system show 2 fires and 1 injury each. Focus your inspection on the transmission, engine, and electrical system.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 65 reports, or 38% of all complaints, with 1 crash and no fires. Check the transmission and drivetrain carefully on a test drive.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The second most common concern: 47 reports (27% of complaints), with no crashes or fires reported. Have the engine inspected before buying.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 37 reports (21% of complaints), including 2 fires and 1 injury. Verify the electronics and check for any signs of electrical trouble.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: 11 reports (6% of complaints), but with 3 crashes reported. Take any steering symptoms seriously on a test drive.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 11 reports (6% of complaints), including 2 fires and 1 injury. Check how the car starts and runs, especially after fueling up.
